ops.text: Self-contained interface compilation to fix Unresolved Reference error in CI

ops.find_block:
echo "⚔️ FORGING (Kotlin -> Bytecode)..."
# Fix: Don't include DynamicEntry.kt in the source list, it must only be on the classpath
# We assume the shell app was built once so we can steal its classes
SHELL_CLASSES="app/build/tmp/kotlin-classes/release"

$WORKDIR/kotlinc/bin/kotlinc \
    dynamic_src/ \
    -cp "$CP:$SHELL_CLASSES" \
    -Xplugin=$WORKDIR/compose-compiler.jar \
    -d $CLASSES_DIR
ops.replace_with:
echo "⚔️ FORGING (Kotlin -> Bytecode)..."
SUPPORT_DIR="$WORKDIR/support_classes"
mkdir -p $SUPPORT_DIR

# Step 1: Compile the interface only so the payload has a reference
# We do NOT include this in the final DEX to avoid ClassCastException
echo "🛠️ Compiling Interface Reference..."
$WORKDIR/kotlinc/bin/kotlinc \
    app/src/main/java/com/speedster/DynamicEntry.kt \
    -cp "$CP" \
    -d $SUPPORT_DIR

# Step 2: Compile the payload using the interface on the classpath
echo "⚔️ Forging Payload..."
$WORKDIR/kotlinc/bin/kotlinc \
    dynamic_src/ \
    -cp "$CP:$SUPPORT_DIR" \
    -Xplugin=$WORKDIR/compose-compiler.jar \
    -d $CLASSES_DIR

operations.find_block:
echo "⚔️ FORGING (Kotlin -> Bytecode)..."
# Fix: Don't include DynamicEntry.kt in the source list, it must only be on the classpath

echo "🛠️ Compiling Interface Reference..."
$WORKDIR/kotlinc/bin/kotlinc \
    app/src/main/java/com/speedster/DynamicEntry.kt \
    -cp "$CP" \
    -d $SUPPORT_DIR

# Step 2: Compile the payload using the interface on the classpath
echo "⚔️ Forging Payload..."
$WORKDIR/kotlinc/bin/kotlinc \
    dynamic_src/ \
    -cp "$CP:$SUPPORT_DIR" \
    -Xplugin=$WORKDIR/compose-compiler.jar \
    -d $CLASSES_DIR

ops.text: Fix Manifest package error and resolve D8 desugaring warnings

ops.find_block:
echo "🔩 CRUNCHING (Bytecode -> Dex)..."
BUILD_TOOLS_DIR=$(ls -d ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/build-tools/34.* | head -1)

$BUILD_TOOLS_DIR/d8 \
    --output $DEX_DIR/ \
    --lib ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platforms/android-34/android.jar \
    $(find $CLASSES_DIR -name "*.class")

echo "🎨 PACKAGING RESOURCES..."
# We use aapt2 to link the compiled DEX and any resources into a dummy APK
# This allows Android's AssetManager to 'see' drawables inside the file.
$BUILD_TOOLS_DIR/aapt2 link \
    -I ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platforms/android-34/android.jar \
    --manifest app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml \
    -o $DEX_DIR/payload.apk \
    $DEX_DIR/classes.dex
ops.replace_with:
echo "🔩 CRUNCHING (Bytecode -> Dex)..."
BUILD_TOOLS_DIR=$(ls -d ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/build-tools/34.* | head -1)

# We include the harvested libs in --lib so d8 can desugar properly
LIB_ARGS=""
for jar in $(find $LIBS_DIR -name "*.jar"); do
    LIB_ARGS="$LIB_ARGS --lib $jar"
done

$BUILD_TOOLS_DIR/d8 \
    --output $DEX_DIR/ \
    --lib ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platforms/android-34/android.jar \
    $LIB_ARGS \
    $(find $CLASSES_DIR -name "*.class")

echo "🎨 PACKAGING RESOURCES..."
$BUILD_TOOLS_DIR/aapt2 link \
    -I ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platforms/android-34/android.jar \
    --manifest app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml \
    --rename-manifest-package com.speedster.payload \
    -o $DEX_DIR/payload.apk \
    $DEX_DIR/classes.dex

operations.find_block:

    --manifest app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml \
    -o $DEX_DIR/payload.apk \
    $DEX_DIR/classes.dex

ops.text: Transform into a Home Launcher and add package query permissions
Eviscerate calculator logic and implement high-performance Dynamic Launcher UI
